items.title: Russian offensive underway against Ukrainian forces in Kursk | REUTERS
items.author: Reuters
items.content:
<article>Russian special forces advanced for miles through a gas pipeline near the town of Sudzha in an attempt to surprise Ukrainian forces as part of a major offensive to expel Ukrainian soldiers from the western Russian region of Kursk.

items.title: Why Canada is jailing more Indigenous people
items.author: Reuters
items.content:
<article>Canada's Prime Minister Justin Trudeau entered office nearly 10 years ago pledging "a total renewal of the relationship between Canada and Indigenous peoples" — including eliminating the over-representation of Indigenous people in custody. 
 
Now, with Trudeau set to step down, those disparities have widened further. Indigenous people, who make up about 5% of Canada's population, account for 34% of Canadians under long-term supervision orders.
 
One of them is Marvin Starblanket, a member of the Mistawasis First Nation who was released from prison two and a half years ago. Correctional Service of Canada rules still govern his life.

#JustinTrudeau #Trudeau #Canada #Indigenous #Prison #Reconciliation

items.title: Greenland
items.author: Reuters
items.content:
<article>In this special episode of the Reuters World News podcast, we visit Greenland – the Arctic island that usually flies under the radar. But since U.S. President Donald Trump recently revived his desire to buy, or take, the autonomous Danish territory, Greenland is taking center stage. His interest and growing Inuit pride have led some locals to view an upcoming vote as a historic chance to free Greenland from Danish influence. We talk to Denmark Senior Correspondent Jacob Gronholt-Pedersen and White House Reporter Trevor Hunnicutt about Greenland’s strategic importance militarily and economically. Plus, as Greenlanders head to the polls, we take a look at the election that needs to thread a needle between Copenhagen and Washington DC. 






Sign up for the Reuters Econ World newsletter here (https://www.reuters.com/newsletters/reuters-econ-world/) .
